faq.label: FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
faq.heading: Your Legal Questions
faq.q1: What types of personal injury cases do you handle?
faq.a1: We handle a wide range of personal injury cases including auto accidents, medical malpractice, nursing home abuse, construction accidents, slip and fall incidents, wrongful death, and more. Our attorneys have over 40 years of combined experience across multiple practice areas.
faq.q2: How long do I have to file a personal injury claim?
faq.a2: In most states, the statute of limitations for personal injury cases is typically two to three years from the date of the accident. However, deadlines can vary by state and case type. It's crucial to consult with an attorney as soon as possible to protect your rights.
faq.q3: How long does a personal injury case typically take?
faq.a3: The timeline varies depending on the complexity of your case, the severity of your injuries, and whether the case settles or goes to trial. Some cases settle within months, while others may take a year or longer. We work efficiently to maximize your compensation while ensuring you receive the best possible outcome.
faq.q4: Do I need to come to your office for a consultation?
faq.a4: While we welcome you to visit our offices, we understand you may be unable to travel due to your injuries. We offer free phone consultations and can visit you at your home or hospital if necessary. Contact us 24/7 to discuss your case.

about.bannerSubtitle: Established in 1981, built on trust, integrity and successfully delivering desired results to our clients.
about.firmHeading: Injury Law Firm With a Proven Track Record
about.firmPara1: Ahmed Law Firm was founded in 1981 with a commitment to providing exceptional legal representation to individuals and families who have been injured due to the negligence of others. Our firm has built a reputation for excellence through decades of dedicated service to our clients throughout New York, including Long Island, Nassau County, Queens County, Kings County, The Bronx, Brooklyn, NYC, and Suffolk County.
about.firmPara2: We understand that facing a personal injury can be overwhelming, both emotionally and financially. That's why we work on a contingency fee basis, meaning you don't pay unless we win your case. Our experienced attorneys are committed to fighting for your rights and securing the compensation you deserve.
about.verdictsPara: Over the years, Ahmed Law Firm has successfully represented thousands of clients and recovered millions of dollars in compensation. Our track record speaks to our dedication and expertise in personal injury law.
about.attorneysHeading: Attorneys Ready To Fight For You
about.attorneysPara: Our legal team brings together diverse expertise and a shared commitment to excellence. Each attorney at Ahmed Law Firm brings unique skills and experience to help achieve the best possible outcomes for our clients.
about.commitmentPara: At Ahmed Law Firm, we believe that every client deserves personalized attention and aggressive representation.
about.experienceIntro: With over 40 years of combined experience, our attorneys have successfully represented thousands of clients across New York.
about.commitmentIntro: We are committed to providing personalized attention and aggressive representation for every client.

abu-ahmed.tagline: Founding attorney with over four decades of experience fighting for injured New Yorkers.
abu-ahmed.bio1: Abu Ahmed founded Ahmed Law Firm in 1981 with a singular mission: to provide exceptional legal representation to individuals and families who have been injured through no fault of their own. For over forty years, he has been a steadfast advocate for victims of negligence throughout New York, including Long Island, Nassau County, Queens County, The Bronx, Brooklyn, NYC, and Suffolk County.
abu-ahmed.bio2: Mr. Ahmed believes that every client deserves personalized attention and aggressive representation. He has built the firm on a foundation of trust, integrity, and an unwavering commitment to securing the compensation his clients need to move forward after life-changing injuries.
abu-ahmed.bio3: Under his leadership, Ahmed Law Firm has recovered millions of dollars for clients in personal injury, medical malpractice, workers' compensation, and wrongful death cases. He takes pride in the firm's contingency fee structure—clients do not pay unless the firm wins their case.
abu-ahmed.highlight2: Over 40 years of experience representing injury victims
abu-ahmed.highlight3: Millions recovered for clients across New York
abu-ahmed.highlight4: Contingency fee basis—no fee unless we win

usman-ahmed.tagline: Dedicated advocate for injury victims, committed to justice and full compensation.
usman-ahmed.bio1: Usman Ahmed brings a sharp legal mind and a deep commitment to client-centered representation at Ahmed Law Firm. He works alongside the firm's founding attorney to serve injured individuals and their families throughout the greater New York area, including Long Island, Nassau County, Queens County, Kings County, The Bronx, Brooklyn, NYC, and Suffolk County.
usman-ahmed.bio2: Mr. Ahmed understands the physical, emotional, and financial toll that serious injuries can take. He focuses on clear communication, thorough case preparation, and aggressive advocacy so that clients can focus on recovery while the firm fights for the compensation they deserve.
usman-ahmed.bio3: His practice spans the full range of personal injury and related matters—from car accidents and slip-and-fall cases to medical malpractice and workers' compensation. He is committed to the firm's core values: no fees unless we win, and no client left without a voice.
usman-ahmed.highlight1: Focused practice in personal injury and workers' rights
usman-ahmed.highlight3: Serving clients across the New York metro area
usman-ahmed.highlight4: Dedicated to securing maximum compensation for victims

faq.bannerSubtitle: Answers to common questions about personal injury claims and your legal rights in New York
faq.intro: Get answers to common questions about personal injury claims in New York. If you don't see your question below, contact us for a free consultation.
faq.footerText: Have more questions? We're here to help.
faq.q1: What is the average settlement for a car accident in NY?
faq.a1: Settlement amounts vary widely based on the severity of injuries, medical costs, lost wages, and other factors. Our attorneys can evaluate your case and give you an idea of what similar cases have recovered. We work to maximize your compensation.
faq.q2: How much is my personal injury case worth?
faq.a2: Case value depends on your injuries, medical treatment, impact on your life, and the liable party's insurance or assets. We work on a contingency fee basis—you don't pay unless we win. Contact us for a free case evaluation.
faq.q3: How long does a personal injury case take?
faq.a3: Some cases settle in months; others take a year or more, especially if they go to trial. We work efficiently while making sure we don't settle for less than you deserve. We keep you updated at every step.
faq.q4: How long do I have to file a personal injury claim?
faq.a4: In New York, you generally have three years from the date of injury to file a personal injury lawsuit. Some claims have shorter deadlines. It's important to speak with an attorney as soon as possible so you don't miss a deadline.
faq.q5: What types of personal injury cases do you handle?
faq.a5: We handle auto accidents, medical malpractice, construction accidents, slip and fall, wrongful death, truck accidents, and more. Our attorneys have decades of experience across multiple practice areas.
faq.q6: Do I need to come to your office for a consultation?
faq.a6: We offer free phone consultations and can meet you at home or in the hospital if you can't travel. Contact us 24/7 to discuss your case.

construction-accidents.bannerSubtitle: Representing construction workers and visitors injured on job sites

0.heading: What to Do After a Construction Accident
0.intro: Construction site injuries can give you rights to workers' comp and sometimes a third-party lawsuit. Consider these steps:

0.text: Notify your employer and, if applicable, the site owner or general contractor as required.

1.text: Get treatment and follow your doctor's orders. Document all visits and restrictions.

2.text: If possible, take photos of the area, equipment, and your injuries. Note what caused the accident.

3.text: Submit a claim to the Workers' Compensation Board. Deadlines apply.

4.text: Keep all medical records, pay stubs, and correspondence with employers and insurers.

5.text: You may have claims against multiple parties; we can evaluate your case.

1.heading: How to File a Claim
1.intro: Our experienced attorneys can guide you through every step of the process. We help with:

listItems.1: Filing claims with all relevant parties and insurers
listItems.3: Calculating damages including medical expenses, lost wages, and pain and suffering
listItems.4: Representing you in court if a fair settlement cannot be reached

slip-fall.bannerTitle: New York Slip and Fall Lawyers
slip-fall.bannerSubtitle: Holding property owners accountable for dangerous conditions
slip-fall.contentTitle: New York Slip and Fall Lawyers

0.heading: What to Do After a Slip and Fall
0.intro: If you were injured in a slip and fall, these steps can help protect your claim:

0.text: Get evaluated and treated. Document your injuries and follow your doctor's advice.

1.text: Report to the property owner or manager and ask for a written incident report.

2.text: Take photos of the condition that caused the fall (e.g., wet floor, uneven surface) and your injuries.

3.text: Collect names and contact details of anyone who saw the fall or the condition.

4.text: Keep your clothing and shoes if they were damaged. Save all medical and incident paperwork.

5.strong: Contact a slip and fall lawyer:
5.text: Premises liability claims require proving the owner knew or should have known of the hazard; we can help.

1.heading: How to File a Claim
1.intro: Our experienced attorneys can guide you through every step of the process. We help with:

listItems.1: Filing claims with all relevant parties and insurers
listItems.3: Calculating damages including medical expenses, lost wages, and pain and suffering
listItems.4: Representing you in court if a fair settlement cannot be reached

2.heading: Common Causes of Slip and Fall Injuries
2.intro: Slip and fall accidents are often caused by:

listItems.2: Poor lighting in stairwells and walkways
listItems.4: Ice and snow on sidewalks and parking lots
listItems.5: Unmarked steps or changes in elevation

3.heading: Types of Injuries We Handle

premises-liability.bannerSubtitle: Fighting for those injured by unsafe property conditions

0.heading: What to Do After a Premises Injury
0.intro: If you were hurt on someone else's property due to a dangerous condition, consider these steps:

0.text: Get treatment and document all injuries and follow-up care.

1.text: Report to the property owner or manager and request a written incident report.

2.text: Take photos of the condition that caused your injury (e.g., broken step, poor lighting, debris).

3.text: Collect names and contact details of anyone who saw the condition or your fall.

4.text: Keep clothing, shoes, and any incident or medical paperwork.

5.text: We can help you prove the owner knew or should have known of the danger and pursue full compensation.

1.heading: How to File a Claim
1.intro: Our experienced attorneys can guide you through every step of the process. We help with:

listItems.1: Filing claims with all relevant parties and insurers
listItems.3: Calculating damages including medical expenses, lost wages, and pain and suffering
listItems.4: Representing you in court if a fair settlement cannot be reached

2.heading: Common Causes and Contributing Factors
2.intro: Injuries in this area can result from a variety of causes. Our attorneys investigate the facts of your case to identify responsible parties and hold them accountable.

listItems.2: Lack of proper training or supervision
listItems.3: Failure to follow safety rules and regulations

workplace-injuries.bannerSubtitle: Helping injured workers secure workers' compensation and more

0.heading: What to Do After a Workplace Injury
0.intro: To protect your right to workers' compensation and any other claims, take these steps:

0.text: Notify your employer as soon as possible. Timely notice is required under New York law.

1.text: Get the care you need. Follow your employer's rules regarding authorized providers if applicable.

2.text: Write down what happened, where, and who was present. Take photos if you can.

3.text: Submit a C-3 form to the Workers' Compensation Board. Deadlines apply.

4.text: Save all medical bills, pay stubs, and correspondence with your employer and the carrier.

5.text: We can help you file, appeal denials, and secure the benefits you deserve.

listItems.2: Lack of proper training or supervision
listItems.3: Failure to follow safety rules and regulations

workers-compensation.bannerSubtitle: Helping injured workers secure the benefits they deserve

0.heading: What to Do After a Workplace Injury
0.intro: To protect your right to workers' compensation benefits in New York, take these steps:

0.text: Notify your employer as soon as possible. New York law requires timely notice.

1.text: Get the treatment you need. Your employer or their carrier may have a list of authorized providers.

2.text: Write down what happened, where, when, and who was present. Take photos if you can.

3.text: Submit a C-3 form (Employee Claim) to the New York Workers' Compensation Board. Deadlines apply.

4.text: Save all medical bills, pay stubs, and correspondence with your employer and the insurance carrier.

5.text: An attorney can help you file correctly, appeal denials, and maximize your benefits.

1.heading: How to File a Workers' Compensation Claim
1.intro: Navigating the claims process can be complex. We assist with:

listItems.0: Filing your C-3 and other forms with the Workers' Compensation Board
listItems.1: Communicating with your employer and the insurance carrier
listItems.2: Appealing denials and securing medical and wage benefits
listItems.4: Representing you at hearings and before the Board

2.heading: Common Causes and Contributing Factors
2.intro: Injuries in this area can result from a variety of causes. Our attorneys investigate the facts of your case to identify responsible parties and hold them accountable.

listItems.2: Lack of proper training or supervision
listItems.3: Failure to follow safety rules and regulations
